deep_dive.title: Differentiable Routability-Driven Package Floorplanning with Pin Assignment
deep_dive.abstract: As advanced packaging technology evolves, increasing interconnect density in redistribution layers (RDLs) makes routability critical to package floorplanning. Meanwhile, power integrity requirements often reserve fan-in regions for the power delivery network (PDN), forcing signal nets through fan-out regions and complicating routability estimation. Existing uniform grid-based congestion models cannot accurately characterize fan-out congestion, while previous pin assignment methods struggle to evaluate net crossings. We propose a differentiable routability-driven floorplanning and pin assignment algorithm for advanced packaging with fan-out routing. First, a differentiable wirelength minimization method directly models discrete chip orientations and back-propagates wirelength gradients to chip locations and orientations. It reduces wirelength under fixed pin selection while avoiding the bias of continuous-angle modeling. Second, a crossing-aware pin assignment method incorporates net-crossing cost into a multi-strategy DPSO algorithm and uses GPU-parallel cost evaluation to reduce wirelength efficiently. Finally, a differentiable routability maximization method constructs a congestion model tailored to fan-out routing and establishes a back-propagation path from congestion information to chip locations, thereby guiding routability optimization. Experimental results show that our method achieves 100% routability on all benchmarks. For cases successfully routed by the baselines, it reduces wirelength by up to approximately 23% compared with a leading floorplanning method equipped with our pin assignment flow.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differentiable routability-driven floorplanning with pin assignment for advanced packaging considering PDN constraints
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对先进封装考虑PDN约束的可微分布线驱动布局规划与引脚分配
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对先进封装考虑PDN约束的可微分布线驱动布局规划与引脚分配
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对先进封装考虑PDN约束的可微分布线驱动布局规划与引脚分配
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a differentiable algorithm for package floorplanning that optimizes routability and pin assignment while respecting power delivery network constraints, directly modeling chip orientations for fan-out routing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 先进封装中互连密度增加使布线能力成为布局规划的关键，而电源完整性要求PDN占用扇入区域，信号线被迫通过扇出区域，现有拥塞模型无法准确评估。本文提出一种可微分的布线驱动布局规划与引脚分配算法，通过直接建模芯片方向和线长最小化，有效处理扇出布线拥塞。实验表明该方法在优化布线能力和电源完整性协同方面优于传统方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 先进封装中互连密度增加使布线能力成为布局规划的关键，而电源完整性要求PDN占用扇入区域，信号线被迫通过扇出区域，现有拥塞模型无法准确评估。本文提出一种可微分的布线驱动布局规划与引脚分配算法，通过直接建模芯片方向和线长最小化，有效处理扇出布线拥塞。实验表明该方法在优化布线能力和电源完整性协同方面优于传统方法。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: SI/PI analysis for 2.5D 3D and Chiplet packaging

quick_skim.title: OSNR/GSNR Prediction in Brownfield Links via a DLM-Anchored Hybrid Physics/ML Model
quick_skim.abstract: We present a DLM-anchored hybrid physics/ML framework for brownfield optical links that accurately predicts per-channel power, OSNR, and GSNR. Calibrating span/ILA boundaries via DLM yields OSNR/GSNR errors of no more than 0.39/0.43 dB across single-channel and OSaaS provisioning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Hybrid physics/ML model for signal quality prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 混合物理/机器学习模型用于信号质量预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 混合物理/机器学习模型用于信号质量预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 混合物理/机器学习模型用于信号质量预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a DLM-anchored hybrid physics/ML framework for OSNR/GSNR prediction in brownfield optical links, achieving high accuracy. The methodology of combining physics and ML is transferable to SI/PI optimization for high-speed interconnects.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对光链路中OSNR/GSNR预测问题，提出了一种基于数字孪生（DLM）的混合物理/机器学习框架。通过校准跨段和ILA边界，模型在单通道和OSaaS场景下实现了不超过0.39/0.43 dB的误差。这种混合建模思路可迁移至高速数字系统的信号与电源完整性优化，为机器学习辅助的SI/PI分析提供了方法论参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对光链路中OSNR/GSNR预测问题，提出了一种基于数字孪生（DLM）的混合物理/机器学习框架。通过校准跨段和ILA边界，模型在单通道和OSaaS场景下实现了不超过0.39/0.43 dB的误差。这种混合建模思路可迁移至高速数字系统的信号与电源完整性优化，为机器学习辅助的SI/PI分析提供了方法论参考。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Machine learning methods for SI/PI analysis and optimization
